seoTitle: What Are Power Automate Cloud and Desktop Flows
metaDescription: Learn the difference between Power Automate Cloud Flows and Desktop Flows, and when to use each for business automation.
intro: Power Automate offers two distinct flow types, Cloud Flows and Desktop Flows, each suited to different automation scenarios. This guide explains what each one does and how to decide which fits your task.

steps.heading: Understand what Cloud Flows are
steps.body: Cloud Flows automate online services and apps, triggered by events like a new email or file upload, running entirely in the cloud.

steps.heading: Understand what Desktop Flows are
steps.body: Desktop Flows use robotic process automation to interact with desktop applications, simulating clicks and keystrokes on a local machine.

steps.heading: Compare Cloud Flows vs Desktop Flows
steps.body: Cloud Flows work best for connector-based online automation, while Desktop Flows handle legacy or desktop-only applications that lack an API.

steps.heading: See real-world automation examples
steps.body: Combine both flow types to automate end-to-end processes that span online services and desktop-only software.

faq.question: When should I use a Desktop Flow instead of a Cloud Flow?
faq.answer: Desktop Flows are best when you need to automate a desktop application that doesn't have a connector or API available.

faq.question: Can Cloud Flows and Desktop Flows work together?
faq.answer: Yes, a Cloud Flow can trigger a Desktop Flow, letting you combine online automation with desktop-level actions.

faq.question: What is attended vs unattended automation?
faq.answer: Attended automation runs with a user present to assist, while unattended automation runs on its own without human interaction.

faq.question: Do Desktop Flows require different licensing?
faq.answer: Desktop Flows can have different licensing considerations than Cloud Flows, so it's worth checking current Power Automate pricing details.
